question.text: Which document should a retirement adviser recommend a client prepare to avoid disputes over asset distribution after death?

answer.text: Only nominations in all financial accounts
A comprehensive and registered Will along with aligned nominations
An informal letter to family members about asset wishes
A verbal communication to the eldest family member

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A registered Will provides the strongest legal standing for asset distribution. Aligning nominations with Will provisions avoids confusion and legal disputes among heirs. **The Trap:** Nominations alone (Option A) do not determine ownership — they only facilitate transfer. Informal letters (Option C) have no legal standing. **Cross-Exam Context:** Will preparation and nomination alignment are key advisory areas in Series XVII and X-B (estate planning). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Recommend annual review of the Will whenever there are life events (birth, death, marriage, divorce, new assets). Ensure all nominations across bank, MF, NPS, and insurance are consistent with the Will.
